title: FIR and Zero FIR guidance
summary: Useful when the user wants to know how to report a cognizable offence, where to file, or what the police must record.

other_laws.title: Information in cognizable cases
other_laws.details: Traditionally used for FIR registration guidance and often cited in legacy practice.

other_laws.title: Statements and confessions before Magistrate
other_laws.details: Relevant when a victim or witness statement needs stronger evidentiary recording.

victim_guidance: Ask for a written acknowledgement, diary number, or FIR number after the complaint is recorded.
If the incident happened elsewhere, request a Zero FIR so the matter can be transferred later.
Carry identity proof, incident timeline, screenshots, photographs, and witness details if available.
police_guidance: Record the basic facts, date, place, accused details if known, and preserve supporting material supplied by the complainant.
When territorial jurisdiction is uncertain but the allegations disclose a cognizable offence, consider Zero FIR workflow as per prevailing procedure.
Separate the immediate safety response from later detailed investigation to avoid delay in registration.
notes: Indian criminal procedure changed in 2024, so current station practice should be cross-checked with BNSS and local police circulars.

title: Theft
summary: Movable property taken dishonestly without consent.

ipc_sections.title: Theft
ipc_sections.details: Defines dishonest moving of movable property out of possession without consent.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for theft
ipc_sections.details: General punishment provision for theft allegations.

victim_guidance: Preserve purchase bills, IMEI numbers, serial numbers, and last known location data for stolen devices.
Record the exact place, time, and circumstances of loss, including CCTV locations nearby.
police_guidance: Capture property identifiers early and alert local surveillance or pawn-shop channels where available.
Check CCTV coverage, call detail records, and device-tracking leads promptly in fresh cases.
notes: Snatching with force may require robbery-related sections instead of plain theft.

title: Robbery or dacoity
summary: Property taken by force, fear, or violence; aggravated when several offenders act together.

ipc_sections.title: Robbery
ipc_sections.details: Explains when theft or extortion escalates into robbery due to violence or fear.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for robbery
ipc_sections.details: General punishment section for robbery.

ipc_sections.title: Dacoity
ipc_sections.details: Applies when five or more persons conjointly commit or attempt robbery.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for dacoity
ipc_sections.details: Punishment provision for dacoity.

victim_guidance: Seek immediate medical help if injured and keep the MLC or hospital papers.
Write down weapon details, vehicle numbers, direction of escape, and distinct physical features.
police_guidance: Treat scene preservation, weapon description, CCTV capture, and medico-legal examination as priority steps.
Consider test identification and route mapping where multiple offenders or vehicles are involved.
notes: Where force or threat is clearly alleged, robbery sections are usually more appropriate than only theft.

title: Cheating, fraud, or criminal breach of trust
summary: Deception to obtain money or property, or dishonest misuse of entrusted property.

ipc_sections.title: Cheating
ipc_sections.details: Covers dishonest or fraudulent inducement causing delivery of property or harmful action.

ipc_sections.title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
ipc_sections.details: Commonly cited when money or property is obtained through deception.

ipc_sections.title: Criminal breach of trust
ipc_sections.details: Applies where entrusted property is dishonestly misused or converted.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for criminal breach of trust
ipc_sections.details: Punishment provision for breach of trust allegations.

victim_guidance: Preserve bank statements, transfer references, invoices, chats, emails, and the exact false representations made.
Note whether the dishonest intention existed from the beginning, because that often affects section choice.
police_guidance: Separate civil-contract disputes from allegations showing initial dishonest inducement or misappropriation.
Secure documentary trail early, including account details, communication history, and beneficiary identification.
notes: Purely contractual payment disputes may need closer screening before criminal sections are applied.

title: Cyber fraud, identity theft, or obscene online content
summary: Online impersonation, OTP scams, payment fraud, or unlawful digital publication.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for cheating by personation
ipc_sections.details: Relevant when impersonation is used to deceive.

ipc_sections.title: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
ipc_sections.details: Often paired with digital financial deception cases.

ipc_sections.title: Word, gesture or act intended to insult modesty
ipc_sections.details: Sometimes considered when abusive or insulting digital conduct targets a woman.

other_laws.title: Identity theft
other_laws.details: Relevant for misuse of passwords, digital signatures, or unique identification features.

other_laws.title: Cheating by personation using computer resource
other_laws.details: Common in OTP, fake KYC, and online impersonation scams.

other_laws.title: Obscene electronic material
other_laws.details: Relevant for obscene publication or transmission online.

other_laws.title: Sexually explicit electronic material
other_laws.details: Relevant for sexually explicit digital content.

victim_guidance: Immediately save screenshots, URLs, handles, transaction IDs, UPI IDs, and device logs.
For account compromise, change passwords, enable multi-factor authentication, and alert the bank or platform quickly.
police_guidance: Preserve digital evidence with timestamps, platform URLs, transaction references, and account freeze requests where appropriate.
Escalate time-sensitive financial fraud through cyber response channels quickly because recovery chances fall with delay.
notes: Platform preservation requests and bank freeze steps are often time-sensitive in cyber fraud cases.

title: Simple hurt or assault
summary: Physical violence causing pain or minor injuries.

ipc_sections.title: Assault
ipc_sections.details: Defines gestures or preparations creating apprehension of criminal force.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt
ipc_sections.details: Commonly used for non-grievous physical injuries.

victim_guidance: Take photographs of injuries as early as possible and obtain medical records even for apparently minor hurt.
Note witnesses and any prior threats or motive that explain the attack.
police_guidance: Collect injury documentation promptly and assess whether weapon use or fracture elevates the offence.
Preserve scene details and CCTV before it is overwritten.
notes: Weapon use, fracture, or permanent injury may shift the case toward grievous hurt sections.

title: Grievous hurt or dangerous weapon injury
summary: Serious bodily injury, fracture, disfigurement, or hurt caused with dangerous means.

ipc_sections.title: Grievous hurt
ipc_sections.details: Lists kinds of injuries legally treated as grievous.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for voluntarily causing grievous hurt
ipc_sections.details: Used for serious injuries where a dangerous weapon provision is not more apt.

ipc_sections.title: Grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means
ipc_sections.details: Relevant for knives, corrosives, fire, or other dangerous means.

victim_guidance: Prioritize emergency treatment and preserve all surgery papers, scans, discharge summaries, and photographs.
If a weapon was used, describe it precisely and avoid washing blood-stained clothing if safe to preserve.
police_guidance: Secure the medical opinion on nature of injury and seize clothing or weapon under proper chain of custody.
Assess scene reconstruction and forensic examination where weapon use is alleged.
notes: Medical opinion usually drives whether hurt is treated as simple or grievous.

title: Sexual harassment, outrage of modesty, or insulting modesty
summary: Unwanted sexual conduct, touching, verbal harassment, or conduct targeting a woman's dignity.

ipc_sections.title: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage modesty
ipc_sections.details: Relevant for non-consensual force or contact targeting a woman.

ipc_sections.title: Sexual harassment
ipc_sections.details: Covers unwelcome physical contact, sexual advances, demands, or explicit sexual remarks.

ipc_sections.title: Word, gesture or act intended to insult modesty
ipc_sections.details: Relevant for verbal, gestural, or expressive acts insulting modesty.

victim_guidance: Write down the exact words, location, time, and identities of people present as early as possible.
Preserve messages, CCTV, clothing, and any nearby eyewitness details.
police_guidance: Take a sensitive statement, preserve digital or CCTV evidence, and assess whether the facts disclose higher sexual offence sections.
Consider victim safety, repeat-contact risk, and witness protection needs.
notes: Serious penetrative sexual assault allegations require different and more stringent sections and procedure.

title: Rape or serious sexual assault
summary: Penetrative sexual assault or aggravated sexual offences.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for rape
ipc_sections.details: General punishment provision for rape offences.

ipc_sections.title: Gang rape
ipc_sections.details: Relevant where multiple persons are involved in the sexual assault.

victim_guidance: Seek urgent medical help, and if possible avoid bathing or washing clothes before medical examination.
Request support from a trusted person, legal aid, or counsellor during statement and medical processes.
police_guidance: Prioritize victim safety, medico-legal examination, trauma-informed recording, and strict evidence-preservation steps.
Ensure statements and seizure steps are handled with privacy and statutory sensitivity.
notes: This is a high-priority category where special procedural safeguards apply and immediate response matters.

title: Stalking, voyeurism, or repeated monitoring
summary: Repeated following, contacting, tracking, or capturing private images without consent.

ipc_sections.title: Voyeurism
ipc_sections.details: Relevant for watching or capturing images of a woman engaged in a private act without consent.

ipc_sections.title: Stalking
ipc_sections.details: Covers repeated following, contacting, or monitoring despite disinterest.

victim_guidance: Save call logs, screenshots, location history, social-media URLs, and dates of repeated incidents.
Report urgent risk if the accused is waiting near home, workplace, or school.
police_guidance: Document repeat pattern, digital trail, and proximity behavior because repetition often matters to the offence structure.
Consider urgent protective measures where escalation risk is visible.
notes: Repeated unwanted contact is often legally important, so chronology should be recorded carefully.

title: Kidnapping or abduction
summary: Taking or removing a person unlawfully, including minors and forced movement.

ipc_sections.title: Kidnapping from lawful guardianship
ipc_sections.details: Relevant when a minor or person of unsound mind is taken from lawful guardianship.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for kidnapping
ipc_sections.details: General punishment section for kidnapping.

ipc_sections.title: Abduction
ipc_sections.details: Defines abduction by force or deceitful means.

ipc_sections.title: Kidnapping, abducting or inducing woman to compel marriage, etc.
ipc_sections.details: Relevant where forced marriage or illicit purpose is alleged.

victim_guidance: Share photographs, last-seen location, clothing description, phone numbers, and travel routes immediately.
For minors, keep birth proof or school records ready to establish age.
police_guidance: Treat fresh missing-minor reports urgently with immediate circulation, CCTV tracing, and digital tracking steps.
Age determination and lawful guardianship facts are often central in minor cases.
notes: Minor cases often require especially urgent handling even when motives are initially unclear.

title: Criminal intimidation or threats
summary: Threats to cause injury, death, or reputational harm to force or alarm someone.

ipc_sections.title: Criminal intimidation
ipc_sections.details: Defines threats intended to cause alarm or compel action or inaction.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for criminal intimidation
ipc_sections.details: General punishment section for intimidation.

victim_guidance: Do not delete threatening calls, recordings, messages, or voicemails.
Write down whether the threat linked to money, witness pressure, relationship disputes, or prior violence.
police_guidance: Capture the exact words, medium, timing, and surrounding motive because context affects seriousness.
Assess immediate protection needs where repeated or deadly threats are alleged.
notes: Threats tied to extortion, stalking, or assault may justify additional sections beyond intimidation.

title: Domestic violence, cruelty, or dowry-linked harassment
summary: Cruelty by spouse or relatives, harassment for dowry, or violence within domestic relationships.

ipc_sections.title: Cruelty by husband or relatives of husband
ipc_sections.details: Commonly invoked for cruelty, harassment, or abuse linked to marriage.

ipc_sections.title: Dowry death
ipc_sections.details: Relevant in serious cases involving death connected with dowry cruelty.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for criminal breach of trust
ipc_sections.details: May be relevant in streedhan or entrusted property disputes where dishonest misappropriation is alleged.

other_laws.law: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
other_laws.title: Civil protection and residence remedies
other_laws.details: Useful for protection orders, residence relief, monetary relief, and related support measures.

victim_guidance: Preserve medical papers, prior messages, audio, photographs of injuries, and any demand for dowry or money.
If unsafe, prioritize shelter, emergency contact support, and immediate protection rather than only documenting later.
police_guidance: Screen separately for physical assault, dowry-linked cruelty, intimidation, and streedhan misappropriation.
Document prior incidents and identify whether urgent protection or medical intervention is needed.
notes: Domestic violence matters may involve both criminal provisions and civil protection remedies.

title: Murder or culpable homicide
summary: Fatal violence or conduct causing death.

ipc_sections.title: Culpable homicide
ipc_sections.details: Foundational definition for homicide offences.

ipc_sections.title: Murder
ipc_sections.details: Explains when culpable homicide amounts to murder.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for murder
ipc_sections.details: General punishment provision for murder.

ipc_sections.title: Attempt to murder
ipc_sections.details: Relevant where the victim survives but the act shows murderous intent or knowledge.

victim_guidance: If the victim is alive, emergency treatment and scene preservation should take priority.
Share the last-seen timeline, hostility history, and persons present at or near the scene.
police_guidance: Treat scene control, inquest procedure, forensic recovery, and witness isolation as immediate priorities.
Distinguish between death, attempted murder, and later medical outcome carefully while building the case theory.
notes: Homicide cases require close coordination between scene, medical, and forensic workflows.

title: Rioting, unlawful assembly, or group violence
summary: Violence or force by an unlawful group.

ipc_sections.title: Unlawful assembly
ipc_sections.details: Defines assembly with a common unlawful object.

ipc_sections.title: Rioting
ipc_sections.details: Punishment provision for rioting.

ipc_sections.title: Rioting, armed with deadly weapon
ipc_sections.details: Relevant when participants are armed with deadly weapons.

ipc_sections.title: Every member of unlawful assembly guilty of offence committed in prosecution of common object
ipc_sections.details: Important for group liability analysis.

victim_guidance: List the number of persons, weapons, slogans, vehicles, and video sources if any.
Share separate injury details for each victim because charge framing may differ across participants.
police_guidance: Build the chronology around common object, participation, weapons, and specific overt acts where identifiable.
Use video, drone, CCTV, and independent witnesses to segregate presence from active participation.
notes: Group liability should be assessed carefully rather than assuming identical roles for all participants.

title: House trespass, mischief, or property damage
summary: Illegal entry, damage to property, or vandalism.

ipc_sections.title: Criminal trespass
ipc_sections.details: Defines unlawful entry or unlawful remaining with intent to commit offence or annoy.

ipc_sections.title: House trespass
ipc_sections.details: Relevant for criminal trespass into a building used as human dwelling.

ipc_sections.title: Mischief
ipc_sections.details: Foundational definition for causing wrongful loss or damage to property.

ipc_sections.title: Mischief causing damage
ipc_sections.details: Commonly used punishment provision for property damage.

victim_guidance: Take photos before repairs and preserve bills, valuation papers, and any footage showing the entry or damage.
Note whether the accused entered to threaten, assault, steal, or only damage property.
police_guidance: Document entry mode, damage valuation, scene photographs, and whether linked offences like theft or intimidation are also disclosed.
Secure neighbour CCTV quickly in residential areas.
notes: Entry plus threat, assault, or theft often requires combined section analysis.

title: Extortion, blackmail, or coercive demand
summary: Threats or fear used to force payment, delivery of property, or disclosure of private material.

ipc_sections.title: Extortion
ipc_sections.details: Defines intentionally putting a person in fear of injury to dishonestly induce delivery of property or valuable security.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for extortion
ipc_sections.details: General punishment provision for extortion.

ipc_sections.title: Criminal intimidation
ipc_sections.details: Relevant where threats are used to create alarm or pressure.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for criminal intimidation
ipc_sections.details: Punishment provision where threats are part of the coercion.

other_laws.title: Violation of privacy
other_laws.details: Can become relevant where intimate images or private content are misused online.

other_laws.title: Sexually explicit electronic material
other_laws.details: Relevant where sexually explicit images or videos are circulated or threatened to be circulated.

victim_guidance: Do not delete chats, call recordings, usernames, payment demands, QR codes, bank details, or screenshots of threats.
Avoid further payment if possible and report the threat quickly, especially when intimate images or repeated demands are involved.
police_guidance: Capture the exact threat, demand amount, payment channel, time line, and whether intimate material or violence was used as leverage.
Preserve digital evidence quickly and identify linked bank, wallet, UPI, SIM, or social-media accounts before records change.
notes: Blackmail cases often involve both intimidation and extortion, and online cases may also require IT Act sections.

title: Cyber bullying, online harassment, or fake profile abuse
summary: Repeated abusive online behavior, fake profiles, morphed images, or social-media harassment.

ipc_sections.title: Defamation
ipc_sections.details: May be relevant where false statements harm reputation.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for defamation
ipc_sections.details: Punishment provision for defamation.

ipc_sections.title: Criminal intimidation
ipc_sections.details: Relevant where threats or fear are part of online abuse.

ipc_sections.title: Word, gesture or act intended to insult modesty
ipc_sections.details: Can be relevant in online abuse directed at a woman.

other_laws.title: Identity theft
other_laws.details: Relevant when a fake profile uses another person's identity credentials or information.

other_laws.title: Cheating by personation using computer resource
other_laws.details: Relevant when impersonation is used through online platforms.

other_laws.title: Obscene electronic material
other_laws.details: Relevant where obscene posts or messages are published or sent electronically.

victim_guidance: Save screenshots with timestamps, profile URLs, usernames, post links, and any emails or call logs linked to the abuse.
Block the account only after preserving evidence, and report both to the platform and to the police if there are threats, fake identity, or obscene content.
police_guidance: Preserve URLs, handles, screenshots, and metadata requests early because social-media content can be deleted quickly.
Check whether the abuse includes impersonation, obscenity, threats, stalking, or image manipulation so the correct sections are added.
notes: Online harassment often overlaps with stalking, intimidation, obscenity, or impersonation.
